commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `message` to the default sandbox tool allowlist and adds a focused policy test for that default.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +22. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence end-to-end reproduction was run for the full Telegram symptom. Source inspection does reproduce the allowlist half: current main omits `message` from `DEFAULT_TOOL_ALLOW`, and the sandbox resolver uses that default.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the default sandbox messaging expansion before merge.

target.title: [codex] Allow message tool in default sandbox allowlist
target.body:
## Summary

- Include the `message` tool in the default sandbox allowlist.
- Add regression coverage for the resolved sandbox tool policy.

## Why

Sandboxed agents replying to channel DMs can lose access to the message tool when the default sandbox policy omits it. The reply path already supports tool-based message delivery, so allowing `message` by default keeps sandboxed agents able to communicate while the existing denylist continues to block explicit channel IDs and gateway-style tools.

Fixes #88253

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/tool-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/sandbox-explain.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: yu-xin-c
